summary: `tesla relay {start,stop,status,doctor}` subcommand tree managing the external lotharbach tesla-http-proxy as a detached subprocess (POSIX Setsid). Generates one-off TLS cert at ~/.config/tesla-pp-cli/relay-cert.pem on first start. PID/log/state files under ~/.config/tesla-pp-cli/. Windows graceful-degrade: prints unsupported-platform JSON and exits zero. Doctor runs synthetic vehicle_data round-trip and surfaces the known VCSEC-unsupported limitation.
reason: Hermes is the free path for infotainment commands (charge/climate/honk). The subprocess lifecycle (PID file, log redirect, port allocation, TLS cert) is non-trivial enough that having the CLI manage it is materially better than a 10-step README.
validated_outcome: 25 relay tests + 8 helper unit tests pass; cross-builds clean on linux/darwin/windows; full SIGTERM-then-SIGKILL escalation path tested with 5s grace; idempotent start/stop; install-recipe-on-missing-binary; port-in-use --port hint; subprocess early-exit cleanup; doctor VCSEC warning; verify-mode short-circuit across all 4 subs.
